description: Phase 4 reload prescriptive seed plan. For each bootstrap slot 1..10, lists the lemmas + grammar catalog ids that the slot MUST seed. Counts must satisfy BOOTSTRAP_LADDER's (vocab_min..vocab_max, grammar_min..grammar_max) bounds. The agent reads this from the brief's must_hit.seed_plan field; the gauntlet's mint_budget + coverage_floor steps enforce the count bounds; lemma identity is enforced softly (warning, not block). See docs/phase4-bootstrap-reload-2026-04-29.md.

policy.lemma_enforcement: soft (warn on substitution, document in spec.intent)
policy.scene_class_enforcement: soft (forbid.py uses it as the default; agent may override with §G override)
policy.override_path: Burn one of the session's §G overrides; document the swap in the spec's intent paragraph

1.intent_seed: A child wakes up; mother is already in the kitchen. The child sees something concrete (e.g. tea on the table) and walks to it.

vocab_seed.meaning: tea (Phase 4.1 honorific-prefix tokenization landed 2026-04-29 — お茶 is now correctly tokenized as a single noun)
hold/carry (manipulation verb; without it the slot has no transitive action)

1.grammar_seed_amendment_2026-04-29: ADDED の(possessive)/へ(direction)/も(also). DROPPED で(means/instrument) — story 1 doesn't naturally need it; deferred to story 2 where the buying scene needs 「お金で」.
1.rationale: Story 1 is the cold start. Loads home + kitchen scene, two characters (私 + 母), four sensory adjectives (warm/bright/big/small) so future stories can describe instead of equate, and the foundational particle/copula/adjective scaffolding. Concrete-color adjectives are deferred to story 3 when colors are diegetically motivated.

2026-04-29: Story 1 grammar_seed: ADDED N5_no_possessive, N5_he_direction, N5_mo_also; DROPPED N5_de_means (deferred to story 2). Reason: first author cycle proved that any natural opening sentence in JP requires possessive-の and direction-へ, which the original 8-point seed plan undercounted.
Story 1 vocab_seed: ADDED 持ちます (manipulation verb without which the narrator can only see, never act). 持ちます stays in.

Story 1 vocab_seed (Phase 4.1, 2026-04-29): RESTORED お茶 after the honorific-prefix tokenization fix landed in pipeline/text_to_story.py merge_tokens Rule 0a. The temporary 茶-only fallback is no longer needed; W00010 was migrated 茶→お茶 in place.
Story 1 ladder: grammar_max 8→10, grammar_min 6→8 to accommodate the 3-point grammar additions.
Story 2 should now consume N5_de_means (お金でりんごを買います) as part of its buying scene, in addition to its prior seed.

2.intent_seed: After breakfast the child walks to the corner shop with mother to buy bread (or fruit). The errand introduces directionality and a transaction without a counter scene.
2.grammar_seed_amendment_2026-04-29: DROPPED N5_no_possessive (now in story 1) and N5_e_direction (now in story 1 as N5_he_direction). ADDED N5_de_means (deferred from story 1) — naturally fits the buying scene as 「お金で」.
2.rationale: Departure → walk → shop. Introduces accompaniment (と), origin (から), and means (で 'with [money]'). Reinforces 私+母+歩きます+の+へ from story 1.

3.intent_seed: Mother shows the child how to crack an egg; the child watches the yellow yolk on the white plate. Concrete colors are diegetically motivated (the food has them).
3.rationale: Past tense lands here so subsequent stories can reflect on what just happened. Te-form unlocks te-iru/te-kudasai downstream. Concrete colors (白い/黄色い) appear ON food so they're never floating poetry.

4.intent_seed: First day: the child is at school; teacher writes name on the board; another student waves. A bounded social scene with a third character.
4.rationale: Negation (masen) lands here — every subsequent story can use 'didn't / isn't.' Te-imasu state lets stories show ongoing actions ('teacher is writing'). Spatial 前/後ろ unlock positional storytelling.

5.intent_seed: The child waits with mother for the train; an old woman drops something; the child picks it up and gives it back. Dialogue happens.
5.rationale: Te-kudasai (request) plus 言います make actual dialogue possible — story 5 is the first with quoted speech. Kara-because lets stories have reasons. Wait/stand verbs unlock pacing in subsequent stories.

6.intent_seed: Sitting on a park bench with grandmother; she reads a book; the child notices something under the bench. Plain forms make relative clauses possible.
6.rationale: Plain forms (G055/N5_dictionary_form) finally enter the corpus, with downstream stories already planned to reuse them in relative-clause constructions. Spatial 下/そば unlock 'under the bench'-style positioning. が-but and でも provide the contrast/concession patterns.

7.intent_seed: Caught in the rain at the doorway of a building; a stranger offers a towel. Sensory + small kindness, no anchor handover.

vocab_seed.lemma: ありがとうございます

7.rationale: Plain copula (だ) and na-adjectives complete the predicate paradigm. Te-iru as a separate intro from the verb te-form gives state predicates.

8.intent_seed: At the bookshop; the child wants to buy a small book but doesn't have enough money. The shopkeeper smiles and asks a question.
8.rationale: Question particle ends the corpus's hedge-による-思います cycle. Counters land here so the next stories can quantify naturally.

9.intent_seed: The child calls their friend who couldn't come to school; reports something that happened. Pure dialogue scene; no anchor object.
9.rationale: Quotative-と enables real reported speech, distinct from the 言います-only pattern story 5 used. いつ for time questions.

10.intent_seed: Evening: the child stands on the roof of the apartment building; clouds. A reflective bookend to the bootstrap. Optional second character.
10.rationale: Sentence-final particles complete the conversational paradigm. Time-of-day 夕方 + adverb ゆっくり let stories pace themselves. Lightest slot in the ladder; closes the bootstrap.
